title: pteridium centrali-africanum
intro: Pteridium centrali-africanum, also known as the Central African brake fern, is a type of fern native to tropical Africa, characterized by its large, leathery fronds. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pteridium centrali-africanum include the potential treatment of conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a diuretic to aid in the removal of excess fluids from the body. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, with the roots and rhizomes being used to make a poultice for topical application.
